tools.name: get_workspace_summary
tools.description: Return compact application, flow, and bot summary without variables or full graph details.

tools.name: get_variable_context
tools.description: Search variables by scope/query. Values are omitted unless includeValues is true.

tools.name: get_application_context
tools.description: Return application-level automation context: flows, bots, and variables.

tools.name: get_module_catalog
tools.description: Return a compact installed and marketplace module index with names, descriptions, actions, and triggers.

tools.name: get_module_details
tools.description: Return full details for one selected module, including action input fields, trigger config, manual setup fields, and installed action references.

tools.name: get_flow_context
tools.description: Return compact flow graph topology and block summaries without full block data or action configs.

tools.name: get_block_details
tools.description: Return full details for one selected block, including block data, action configs, HTTP bodies, custom code files, triggers, menu payloads, and media paths.

tools.name: get_action_schema
tools.description: Return supported action kinds and important invariants for draft/apply flow edits.

tools.name: get_design_guidelines
tools.description: Return shared flow-design guidance and examples used by both AI prompt assembly and MCP consumers.

tools.name: search_flow_examples
tools.description: Search compact reusable flow examples by keyword or tag for common business cases.

tools.name: get_flow_example
tools.description: Return one reusable flow example by id, optionally including its full action-batch schema example.

tools.name: validate_actions
tools.description: Dry-run validation of a proposed batch of flow-builder actions without mutating state. OPTIONAL: apply_actions runs this same validation itself and applies nothing when invalid, so calling validate_actions first is redundant — use it only to check a draft you do not intend to apply yet.

tools.name: apply_actions
tools.description: Validate and apply a batch of flow-builder actions. Validation always runs first; when it fails, nothing is applied and the same errors validate_actions would return are reported. Call this directly — a separate validate_actions call beforehand is unnecessary.

tools.name: list_broadcasts
tools.description: List broadcasts in the application, with status, schedule, and delivery counts. Supports filtering by status/bot/recurrence and pagination.

tools.name: get_broadcast_details
tools.description: Return full details for a single broadcast: status, schedule, recurrence, linked flow, and delivery breakdown by status.

tools.name: get_broadcast_analytics
tools.description: Return analytics/stats for a broadcast: delivery breakdown by status plus per-message-block sent/clicked stats for its flow over an optional date window.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_broadcast_analytics · Private data: get_block_details · Public sink: get_broadcast_details, get_broadcast_analytics · Destructive: validate_actions.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_broadcast_analytics · Private data: get_block_details · Public sink: get_broadcast_details, get_broadcast_analytics · Destructive: validate_actions

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
